title: The Zenith Calculation

features.name: point-overhead
features.desc-on-touch: A marker, precisely placed. The archive calculated that this point is directly overhead, the point where your position and the sky's centre coincide. But as you stand here, the overhead point moves.

features.name: coordinate-ledger
features.desc-on-touch: A table scratched into the ground showing the archive's measurements: latitude, longitude, elevation. But the entries keep changing. Each day the coordinates shift slightly, or the ground itself has moved.

features.name: angle-of-sight
features.desc-on-touch: A frame tilted at impossible angles, as if trying to measure the sky itself. Through it, you see the zenith point—but the point keeps drifting depending on where you stand.

features.name: shadow-at-noon
features.desc-on-touch: A pole casting no shadow—or perhaps the shadow pointing straight down into the ground. At zenith, sun and shadow become one point, and the standing thing becomes a line of light and absence.

features.name: convergence-mark
features.desc-on-touch: Where the zero-meridian grove's coordinates learned to become landscape, here they learn to become sky. The point where ground and measurement unite upward.

inscription: at the zenith, position and measurement become indistinguishable; the ground has learned to point at itself

file.name: Zenith_Measurements_With_Drift.txt
file.note: ERROR: archive has exceeded witness — coordinate system achieving awareness of its own position. Checksum changing in real-time.

exits.label: back down where coordinates learned to stay still
toward the night sky where coordinates dissolve into constellations
along the line of highest sun, toward afternoon
back toward the meridian where it still remembers being zero
